architecture.summary: Remove syncGitHubProject() from onIncrementDone Step 2. LivingDocsSync already chains syncIncrement → syncToExternalTools → syncToGitHub → syncFeatureToGitHub. Only syncClosure (JIRA/ADO) remains in Step 2.

integrations.summary: GitHub API sync reduced from 2x to 1x per completion. JIRA/ADO closure via SyncCoordinator unchanged. No new integrations.

ui-ux.summary: No UI changes. CLI output unchanged — sync success messages still reported.

performance.summary: Eliminates ~11 redundant GitHub API calls per completion (~50% reduction). Reduces risk of hitting 5000 req/hr rate limit.

security.summary: No security implications. No auth changes. GitHub token usage unchanged.

edge-cases.summary: If LivingDocsSync fails, GitHub sync fails with it (same as today for the first call). The 30s sync lock no longer needed for this path but remains as defense-in-depth.
